"""Synchronous alert service for Celery workers.

This is a synchronous version of the AlertService for use in Celery tasks
which run synchronously.
"""

import logging
from datetime import datetime, time, timedelta, timezone
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional

from sqlalchemy import and_, select
from sqlalchemy.orm import Session

from app.models.incident import Incident, IncidentStatus
from app.models.in_app_notification import InAppNotification, NotificationType
from app.models.notification_channel import NotificationChannel
from app.models.notification_preference import NotificationPreference
from app.models.notification_rule import NotificationRule, Severity, TriggerType
from app.models.user import User
from app.services.realtime import RealtimeService

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class SyncAlertService:
    """Synchronous service for processing alerts and creating incidents.

    For use in Celery worker tasks that run synchronously.
    Notifications are queued as separate Celery tasks rather than sent inline.
    """

    def __init__(self, db: Session):
        self.db = db

    def trigger_alert(
        self,
        trigger_type: TriggerType,
        severity: Severity,
        title: str,
        message: str,
        resource_type: Optional[str] = None,
        resource_id: Optional[str] = None,
        details: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
    ) -> List[Incident]:
        """Trigger an alert based on an event.

        This method:
        1. Finds matching NotificationRules for this trigger_type
        2. Checks cooldown (skip if recently triggered)
        3. Creates Incident for each matching rule
        4. Queues notifications to be sent (via Celery task)

        Args:
            trigger_type: The type of event that triggered this alert
            severity: The severity level of the alert
            title: Alert title
            message: Alert description/message
            resource_type: Optional resource type (e.g., "uptime_monitor", "budget")
            resource_id: Optional resource identifier
            details: Additional context details

        Returns:
            List of created Incident objects
        """
        created_incidents: List[Incident] = []

        # Find matching notification rules
        matching_rules = self._find_matching_rules(
            trigger_type=trigger_type,
            severity=severity,
            resource_type=resource_type,
            resource_id=resource_id,
        )

        if not matching_rules:
            logger.info(
                f"No matching notification rules for trigger_type={trigger_type.value}, "
                f"severity={severity.value}"
            )
            # Still create an incident even without matching rules for tracking
            incident = self._create_incident(
                notification_rule_id=None,
                trigger_type=trigger_type.value,
                severity=severity.value,
                title=title,
                message=message,
                resource_type=resource_type,
                resource_id=resource_id,
                details=details,
            )
            created_incidents.append(incident)

            # CRITICAL: Commit incident FIRST before best-effort operations
            # This ensures the incident is persisted even if downstream operations fail
            self.db.commit()

            # Best-effort: dispatch diagnostic tasks for infra incidents
            self._dispatch_diagnostic_tasks(created_incidents)

            # Best-effort operations - failures won't rollback the incident
            self._do_best_effort_notifications(
                incident=incident,
                trigger_type=trigger_type,
                title=title,
                message=message,
                severity=severity.value,
            )

            return created_incidents

        for rule in matching_rules:
            # Check cooldown
            if not self._check_cooldown(rule):
                logger.info(
                    f"Skipping rule {rule.id} ({rule.name}) due to cooldown"
                )
                continue

            # Create incident
            incident = self._create_incident(
                notification_rule_id=rule.id,
                trigger_type=trigger_type.value,
                severity=severity.value,
                title=title,
                message=message,
                resource_type=resource_type,
                resource_id=resource_id,
                details=details,
            )
            created_incidents.append(incident)

            # Update rule's last_triggered and trigger_count
            rule.last_triggered = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
            rule.trigger_count = (rule.trigger_count or 0) + 1

            # Queue notifications to configured channels
            self._queue_notifications(
                rule=rule,
                title=title,
                message=message,
                severity=severity.value,
                details=details,
            )

        # CRITICAL: Commit incidents and rule updates FIRST
        # This ensures incidents are persisted even if downstream operations fail
        self.db.commit()

        logger.info(
            f"Created {len(created_incidents)} incident(s) for "
            f"trigger_type={trigger_type.value}"
        )

        # Best-effort: dispatch diagnostic tasks for infra incidents
        self._dispatch_diagnostic_tasks(created_incidents)

        # Best-effort operations - failures won't rollback the incidents
        for incident in created_incidents:
            self._do_best_effort_notifications(
                incident=incident,
                trigger_type=trigger_type,
                title=title,
                message=message,
                severity=severity.value,
            )

        return created_incidents

    def _dispatch_diagnostic_tasks(self, incidents: List[Incident]):
        """Best-effort: queue diagnostic tasks for infrastructure incidents.

        Only dispatches for host/docker resource types where resource_id
        is the OTel agent ID.
        """
        for incident in incidents:
            try:
                if incident.resource_type in ("host", "docker") and incident.resource_id:
                    from workers.tasks.diagnostic import dispatch_diagnostic_task
                    dispatch_diagnostic_task.delay(incident_id=incident.id)
            except Exception as e:
                logger.warning(
                    f"Failed to queue diagnostic task for incident {incident.id}: {e}"
                )

    def send_recovery_notifications(
        self,
        trigger_type: TriggerType,
        severity: Severity,
        title: str,
        message: str,
        resource_type: Optional[str] = None,
        resource_id: Optional[str] = None,
        details: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
    ) -> bool:
        """Send recovery notifications without creating incidents.

        Used for MONITOR_UP events: sends alerts through configured notification
        channels (email, Slack, etc.) but does NOT create an incident record.
        This keeps the incident list clean (one incident per outage, not two).

        Args:
            trigger_type: The recovery trigger type (e.g., MONITOR_UP)
            severity: The severity level
            title: Notification title
            message: Notification message
            resource_type: Optional resource type
            resource_id: Optional resource identifier
            details: Additional context details

        Returns:
            True if any notifications were sent, False otherwise
        """
        matching_rules = self._find_matching_rules(
            trigger_type=trigger_type,
            severity=severity,
            resource_type=resource_type,
            resource_id=resource_id,
        )

        if not matching_rules:
            logger.info(
                f"No matching notification rules for recovery "
                f"trigger_type={trigger_type.value}, severity={severity.value}"
            )
            return False

        notifications_sent = False
        for rule in matching_rules:
            if not self._check_cooldown(rule):
                logger.info(
                    f"Skipping recovery rule {rule.id} ({rule.name}) due to cooldown"
                )
                continue

            # Queue notifications to configured channels
            self._queue_notifications(
                rule=rule,
                title=title,
                message=message,
                severity=severity.value,
                details=details,
            )

            # Update rule's last_triggered and trigger_count
            rule.last_triggered = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
            rule.trigger_count = (rule.trigger_count or 0) + 1
            notifications_sent = True

        if notifications_sent:
            self.db.commit()
            logger.info(
                f"Sent recovery notifications for trigger_type={trigger_type.value}"
            )

        return notifications_sent

    def _find_matching_rules(
        self,
        trigger_type: TriggerType,
        severity: Severity,
        resource_type: Optional[str] = None,
        resource_id: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> List[NotificationRule]:
        """Find notification rules that match the given event."""
        # Base query: active rules with matching trigger type
        rules = self.db.execute(
            select(NotificationRule).where(
                and_(
                    NotificationRule.is_active == True,
                    NotificationRule.trigger_type == trigger_type,
                )
            )
        ).scalars().all()

        # Filter by severity and conditions
        matching_rules = []
        for rule in rules:
            # Check severity filter
            if rule.severity_filter:
                if severity.value not in rule.severity_filter:
                    continue

            # Check conditions
            if rule.conditions:
                if not self._check_conditions(
                    rule.conditions, resource_type, resource_id
                ):
                    continue

            matching_rules.append(rule)

        return matching_rules

    def _check_conditions(
        self,
        conditions: Dict[str, Any],
        resource_type: Optional[str],
        resource_id: Optional[str],
    ) -> bool:
        """Check if event matches rule conditions."""
        # Check resource_types filter
        if "resource_types" in conditions and conditions["resource_types"]:
            if resource_type not in conditions["resource_types"]:
                return False

        # Check monitor_ids filter
        if "monitor_ids" in conditions and conditions["monitor_ids"]:
            if resource_type == "uptime_monitor" and resource_id:
                try:
                    monitor_id = int(resource_id)
                    if monitor_id not in conditions["monitor_ids"]:
                        return False
                except (ValueError, TypeError):
                    return False

        return True

    def _check_cooldown(self, rule: NotificationRule) -> bool:
        """Check if the rule's cooldown period has elapsed.

        Returns True if alert should proceed (cooldown elapsed or disabled).
        Returns False if still in cooldown period.
        """
        # Cooldown of 0 means disabled - always allow
        cooldown_minutes = rule.cooldown_minutes if rule.cooldown_minutes is not None else 0
        if cooldown_minutes == 0:
            return True

        if not rule.last_triggered:
            return True

        cooldown_delta = timedelta(minutes=cooldown_minutes)
        cooldown_end = rule.last_triggered + cooldown_delta

        return datetime.now(timezone.utc) >= cooldown_end

    def _reset_cooldown_for_incident(self, incident: Incident) -> None:
        """Reset the cooldown for the notification rule associated with an incident.

        When an incident is resolved (manually or auto), the cooldown should be reset
        so that if the same issue occurs again, a new alert will be triggered immediately.
        This prevents recurring issues from being masked by the cooldown period.

        Args:
            incident: The resolved incident whose rule's cooldown should be reset.
        """
        if not incident.notification_rule_id:
            return

        rule = self.db.execute(
            select(NotificationRule).where(
                NotificationRule.id == incident.notification_rule_id
            )
        ).scalar_one_or_none()

        if rule and rule.last_triggered:
            rule.last_triggered = None
            logger.info(
                f"Reset cooldown for notification rule {rule.id} ({rule.name}) "
                f"after resolving incident {incident.id}"
            )

    def _create_incident(
        self,
        notification_rule_id: Optional[int],
        trigger_type: str,
        severity: str,
        title: str,
        message: str,
        resource_type: Optional[str],
        resource_id: Optional[str],
        details: Optional[Dict[str, Any]],
    ) -> Incident:
        """Create a new incident record."""
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        incident = Incident(
            notification_rule_id=notification_rule_id,
            title=title,
            description=message,
            status=IncidentStatus.OPEN,
            severity=severity,
            trigger_type=trigger_type,
            resource_type=resource_type,
            resource_id=resource_id,
            details=details,
            started_at=now,
        )

        self.db.add(incident)
        self.db.flush()  # Get the ID without committing

        logger.info(f"Created incident {incident.id}: {title}")

        return incident

    def _queue_notifications(
        self,
        rule: NotificationRule,
        title: str,
        message: str,
        severity: str,
        details: Optional[Dict[str, Any]],
    ) -> None:
        """Queue notifications to all channels configured in the rule."""
        if not rule.channel_ids:
            logger.warning(f"Rule {rule.id} has no channel_ids configured")
            return

        # Import here to avoid circular imports
        from workers.tasks.notifications import send_notification_task

        for channel_id in rule.channel_ids:
            try:
                # Verify channel exists and is active
                channel = self.db.execute(
                    select(NotificationChannel).where(
                        NotificationChannel.id == channel_id
                    )
                ).scalar_one_or_none()

                if not channel:
                    logger.warning(
                        f"Channel {channel_id} not found for rule {rule.id}"
                    )
                    continue

                if not channel.is_active:
                    logger.info(
                        f"Skipping inactive channel {channel_id} for rule {rule.id}"
                    )
                    continue

                # Build notification details
                notification_details = details.copy() if details else {}
                if rule.include_details:
                    notification_details["rule_name"] = rule.name
                    notification_details["rule_id"] = rule.id

                # Queue the notification as a Celery task
                send_notification_task.delay(
                    channel_id=channel_id,
                    title=title,
                    message=message,
                    severity=severity,
                    details=notification_details,
                )

                logger.info(
                    f"Queued notification for channel {channel_id} "
                    f"({channel.channel_type.value})"
                )

            except Exception as e:
                logger.error(
                    f"Error queueing notification for channel {channel_id}: {e}"
                )

    def auto_resolve_incidents(
        self,
        trigger_type: TriggerType,
        resource_type: str,
        resource_id: str,
        send_notifications: bool = True,
    ) -> List[Incident]:
        """Auto-resolve open incidents when a recovery event occurs.

        When MONITOR_UP is triggered, this resolves both MONITOR_DOWN and
        MONITOR_DEGRADED incidents for the same resource.

        Args:
            trigger_type: The recovery trigger type
            resource_type: The resource type (e.g., "uptime_monitor")
            resource_id: The resource identifier
            send_notifications: Whether to send resolution notifications (default True)
        """
        # Map recovery triggers to their incident triggers
        # MONITOR_UP should resolve both DOWN and DEGRADED incidents
        recovery_map = {
            TriggerType.MONITOR_UP: [TriggerType.MONITOR_DOWN, TriggerType.MONITOR_DEGRADED],
        }

        incident_triggers = recovery_map.get(trigger_type)
        if not incident_triggers:
            return []

        # Find open incidents for this resource (matching any of the incident triggers)
        incident_trigger_values = [t.value for t in incident_triggers]
        incidents = self.db.execute(
            select(Incident).where(
                and_(
                    Incident.status.in_([IncidentStatus.OPEN, IncidentStatus.ACKNOWLEDGED]),
                    Incident.trigger_type.in_(incident_trigger_values),
                    Incident.resource_type == resource_type,
                    Incident.resource_id == resource_id,
                )
            )
        ).scalars().all()

        resolved_incidents = []
        for incident in incidents:
            incident.status = IncidentStatus.RESOLVED
            incident.resolved_at = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
            incident.notes = (
                f"{incident.notes or ''}\n\nAuto-resolved: Recovery detected"
            ).strip()
            resolved_incidents.append(incident)
            logger.info(f"Auto-resolved incident {incident.id}: {incident.title}")

            # Reset cooldown so future alerts can trigger immediately
            self._reset_cooldown_for_incident(incident)

            # Send resolution notification (bypasses cooldown)
            if send_notifications:
                self._send_resolution_notification(
                    incident=incident,
                    resolved_by=None,  # Auto-resolved
                    resolution_notes="Recovery detected automatically",
                )

        if resolved_incidents:
            # CRITICAL: Commit resolution status AND cooldown reset FIRST
            self.db.commit()

            # Best-effort: Create in-app notifications and publish real-time events
            for incident in resolved_incidents:
                try:
                    self._create_in_app_notifications(
                        incident=incident,
                        notification_type=NotificationType.INCIDENT_RESOLVED,
                        title=f"Resolved: {incident.title}",
                        message="Incident has been auto-resolved: Recovery detected",
                        severity="info",
                    )
                    self.db.commit()
                except Exception as e:
                    logger.warning(f"In-app notification for resolution failed: {e}")
                    try:
                        self.db.rollback()
                    except Exception:
                        pass

                try:
                    self._publish_realtime_event(
                        event_type="incident_resolved",
                        incident=incident,
                        title=f"Resolved: {incident.title}",
                        severity="info",
                        trigger_type=incident.trigger_type,
                    )
                except Exception as e:
                    logger.warning(f"Realtime event for resolution failed: {e}")
                # Note: No push notifications for resolutions (info severity, not urgent)

        return resolved_incidents

    def _send_resolution_notification(
        self,
        incident: Incident,
        resolved_by: Optional[str] = None,
        resolution_notes: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> None:
        """Send notification when an incident is resolved.

        Resolution notifications bypass cooldown since users should always
        be informed when issues are fixed.

        Args:
            incident: The resolved incident
            resolved_by: Name of the user who resolved (None for auto-resolve)
            resolution_notes: Optional resolution notes
        """
        # If incident has no notification rule, we can't send notification
        if not incident.notification_rule_id:
            logger.info(
                f"Incident {incident.id} has no notification rule, skipping resolution notification"
            )
            return

        # Get the notification rule
        rule = self.db.execute(
            select(NotificationRule).where(
                NotificationRule.id == incident.notification_rule_id
            )
        ).scalar_one_or_none()

        if not rule:
            logger.warning(
                f"Notification rule {incident.notification_rule_id} not found for incident {incident.id}"
            )
            return

        if not rule.is_active:
            logger.info(
                f"Notification rule {rule.id} is inactive, skipping resolution notification"
            )
            return

        # Build resolution message
        resolver = resolved_by or "System (auto-resolved)"
        title = f"✅ Resolved: {incident.title}"
        message = f"Incident has been resolved by {resolver}."
        if resolution_notes:
            message += f"\n\nNotes: {resolution_notes}"

        # Calculate incident duration
        if incident.started_at and incident.resolved_at:
            duration = incident.resolved_at - incident.started_at
            hours, remainder = divmod(int(duration.total_seconds()), 3600)
            minutes, seconds = divmod(remainder, 60)
            if hours > 0:
                duration_str = f"{hours}h {minutes}m"
            elif minutes > 0:
                duration_str = f"{minutes}m {seconds}s"
            else:
                duration_str = f"{seconds}s"
            message += f"\n\nDuration: {duration_str}"

        details = {
            "incident_id": incident.id,
            "original_severity": incident.severity,
            "original_trigger_type": incident.trigger_type,
            "resource_type": incident.resource_type,
            "resource_id": incident.resource_id,
            "resolved_by": resolver,
            "resolution_type": "auto" if resolved_by is None else "manual",
        }
        if incident.details:
            details["original_details"] = incident.details

        # Queue notifications (bypass cooldown for resolutions)
        self._queue_notifications(
            rule=rule,
            title=title,
            message=message,
            severity="info",  # Resolutions are always info severity
            details=details,
        )

        logger.info(
            f"Queued resolution notification for incident {incident.id} via rule {rule.id}"
        )

    def _do_best_effort_notifications(
        self,
        incident: Incident,
        trigger_type: TriggerType,
        title: str,
        message: str,
        severity: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Perform best-effort notification operations after incident is committed.

        These operations can fail without affecting the incident persistence.
        Each operation is wrapped in its own try-catch to isolate failures.
        """
        # 1. Create in-app notifications (has its own commit)
        try:
            self._create_in_app_notifications(
                incident=incident,
                notification_type=self._map_trigger_to_notification_type(trigger_type),
                title=title,
                message=message,
                severity=severity,
            )
            self.db.commit()
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning(f"In-app notifications failed (non-critical): {e}")
            try:
                self.db.rollback()
            except Exception:
                pass  # Session may already be in a bad state

        # 2. Publish real-time event (non-DB operation)
        try:
            self._publish_realtime_event(
                event_type="incident_created",
                incident=incident,
                title=title,
                severity=severity,
                trigger_type=trigger_type.value,
            )
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning(f"Realtime event failed (non-critical): {e}")

        # 3. Queue push notifications (Celery task)
        try:
            self._queue_push_notifications(
                title=title,
                body=message,
                url=f"/incidents?open={incident.id}",
                severity=severity,
                trigger_type=trigger_type.value,
            )
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning(f"Push notification queueing failed (non-critical): {e}")

    def _map_trigger_to_notification_type(self, trigger_type: TriggerType) -> NotificationType:
        """Map TriggerType to NotificationType for in-app notifications."""
        mapping = {
            TriggerType.MONITOR_DOWN: NotificationType.MONITOR_DOWN,
            TriggerType.MONITOR_UP: NotificationType.MONITOR_UP,
            TriggerType.MONITOR_DEGRADED: NotificationType.MONITOR_DOWN,  # Use monitor_down for degraded too
            TriggerType.SSL_EXPIRING: NotificationType.SSL_EXPIRING,
            TriggerType.SSL_EXPIRED: NotificationType.SSL_EXPIRED,
            TriggerType.BUDGET_THRESHOLD: NotificationType.BUDGET_THRESHOLD,
            TriggerType.BUDGET_EXCEEDED: NotificationType.BUDGET_EXCEEDED,
            TriggerType.HOST_METRIC_THRESHOLD: NotificationType.HOST_ALERT,
            TriggerType.CONTAINER_METRIC_THRESHOLD: NotificationType.CONTAINER_ALERT,
            TriggerType.CLOUDWATCH_METRIC_THRESHOLD: NotificationType.CLOUDWATCH_ALERT,
        }
        return mapping.get(trigger_type, NotificationType.INCIDENT_CREATED)

    def _create_in_app_notifications(
        self,
        incident: Incident,
        notification_type: NotificationType,
        title: str,
        message: str,
        severity: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Create in-app notification records for all active users.

        Respects each user's NotificationPreference (severity and trigger type filters).
        """
        try:
            # Get all active users
            result = self.db.execute(
                select(User).where(User.is_active == True)
            )
            users = list(result.scalars().all())

            # Get all preferences in one query
            pref_result = self.db.execute(
                select(NotificationPreference)
            )
            prefs_by_user = {
                p.user_id: p for p in pref_result.scalars().all()
            }

            for user in users:
                pref = prefs_by_user.get(user.id)

                # Check if user has in-app notifications disabled
                if pref and not pref.in_app_enabled:
                    continue

                # Check severity filter
                if pref and pref.severity_filter:
                    if severity not in pref.severity_filter:
                        continue

                # Check trigger type filter
                if pref and pref.trigger_types:
                    if notification_type.value not in pref.trigger_types:
                        continue

                notification = InAppNotification(
                    user_id=user.id,
                    type=notification_type.value,  # Use .value to get lowercase string for postgres enum
                    title=title,
                    message=message,
                    severity=severity,
                    incident_id=incident.id,
                    resource_type=incident.resource_type,
                    resource_id=incident.resource_id,
                    action_url=f"/incidents?open={incident.id}",
                    details=incident.details,
                )
                self.db.add(notification)

            logger.info(f"Created in-app notifications for {len(users)} users")

        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Failed to create in-app notifications: {e}")

    def _publish_realtime_event(
        self,
        event_type: str,
        incident: Incident,
        title: str,
        severity: str,
        trigger_type: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Publish a real-time event via Redis pub/sub for WebSocket delivery."""
        try:
            RealtimeService.publish_event_sync(
                event_type=event_type,
                data={
                    "incident_id": incident.id,
                    "title": title,
                    "severity": severity,
                    "trigger_type": trigger_type,
                    "resource_type": incident.resource_type,
                    "resource_id": incident.resource_id,
                    "status": incident.status.value if hasattr(incident.status, 'value') else str(incident.status),
                },
                target_user_ids=None,  # Broadcast to all
            )
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Failed to publish real-time event: {e}")

    def _queue_push_notifications(
        self,
        title: str,
        body: str,
        url: str,
        severity: str,
        trigger_type: str,
    ) -> None:
        """Queue browser push notifications via Celery for eligible users."""
        try:
            from workers.tasks.push_notifications import send_push_notification_task

            # Get users who have push enabled and match filters
            now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
            current_time = now.time()

            result = self.db.execute(
                select(NotificationPreference).where(
                    NotificationPreference.push_enabled == True
                )
            )
            preferences = list(result.scalars().all())

            eligible_user_ids = []
            for pref in preferences:
                # Check severity filter
                if pref.severity_filter and severity not in pref.severity_filter:
                    continue

                # Check trigger type filter
                if pref.trigger_types and trigger_type not in pref.trigger_types:
                    continue

                # Check quiet hours
                if pref.quiet_hours_start and pref.quiet_hours_end:
                    quiet_start = pref.quiet_hours_start
                    quiet_end = pref.quiet_hours_end

                    # Handle overnight quiet hours (e.g., 22:00 - 07:00)
                    if quiet_start > quiet_end:
                        # Quiet hours span midnight
                        if current_time >= quiet_start or current_time <= quiet_end:
                            continue
                    else:
                        # Normal quiet hours (e.g., 09:00 - 17:00)
                        if quiet_start <= current_time <= quiet_end:
                            continue

                eligible_user_ids.append(pref.user_id)

            if eligible_user_ids:
                send_push_notification_task.delay(
                    user_ids=eligible_user_ids,
                    title=title,
                    body=body,
                    url=url,
                    severity=severity,
                )
                logger.info(f"Queued push notifications for {len(eligible_user_ids)} users")
            else:
                logger.info("No eligible users for push notifications")

        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Failed to queue push notifications: {e}")


# Convenience function for use in worker tasks
def trigger_alert_sync(
    db: Session,
    trigger_type: TriggerType,
    severity: Severity,
    title: str,
    message: str,
    resource_type: Optional[str] = None,
    resource_id: Optional[str] = None,
    details: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
) -> List[Incident]:
    """Convenience function to trigger an alert synchronously.

    Usage in worker tasks:
        from app.services.alert_service_sync import trigger_alert_sync
        from app.models.notification_rule import TriggerType, Severity

        with sync_db_session() as db:
            trigger_alert_sync(
                db=db,
                trigger_type=TriggerType.MONITOR_DOWN,
                severity=Severity.CRITICAL,
                title=f"Monitor Down: {monitor.name}",
                message=f"URL {monitor.url} is not responding",
                resource_type="uptime_monitor",
                resource_id=str(monitor.id),
                details={"url": monitor.url, "error": error_message},
            )
    """
    service = SyncAlertService(db)
    return service.trigger_alert(
        trigger_type=trigger_type,
        severity=severity,
        title=title,
        message=message,
        resource_type=resource_type,
        resource_id=resource_id,
        details=details,
    )


def send_resolution_notification_sync(
    db: Session,
    incident: Incident,
    resolved_by: Optional[str] = None,
    resolution_notes: Optional[str] = None,
) -> None:
    """Send resolution notification synchronously.

    Use this when manually resolving incidents in synchronous code.

    Args:
        db: Database session
        incident: The resolved incident
        resolved_by: Name of the user who resolved (None for auto-resolve)
        resolution_notes: Optional resolution notes
    """
    service = SyncAlertService(db)
    service._send_resolution_notification(
        incident=incident,
        resolved_by=resolved_by,
        resolution_notes=resolution_notes,
    )

1→import { useEffect, useRef, useState, useCallback } from 'react'
     2→import { useSession } from 'next-auth/react'
     3→import { useQueryClient } from '@tanstack/react-query'
     4→import { io, Socket } from 'socket.io-client'
     5→import { useToast } from '@/components/Toast'
     6→
     7→interface WebSocketEvent {
     8→  incident_id: number
     9→  title: string
    10→  severity: string
    11→  trigger_type: string
    12→  resource_type?: string
    13→  resource_id?: string
    14→  status?: string
    15→}
    16→
    17→export function useWebSocket() {
    18→  const { data: session, status: sessionStatus } = useSession()
    19→  const queryClient = useQueryClient()
    20→  const toast = useToast()
    21→  const socketRef = useRef<Socket | null>(null)
    22→  const [isConnected, setIsConnected] = useState(false)
    23→
    24→  const invalidateNotificationQueries = useCallback(() => {
    25→    queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['notifications'] })
    26→    queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['unreadCount'] })
    27→  }, [queryClient])
    28→
    29→  const invalidateIncidentQueries = useCallback(() => {
    30→    queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['incidents'] })
    31→    queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['incidentSummary'] })
    32→  }, [queryClient])
    33→
    34→  useEffect(() => {
    35→    if (sessionStatus !== 'authenticated' || !session?.accessToken) {
    36→      return
    37→    }
    38→
    39→    // In production, Socket.IO goes through nginx (same origin).
    40→    // In development (port 3000), Next.js rewrites don't support WebSocket
    41→    // upgrades, so we connect directly to the backend on port 8000.
    42→    const isDev = typeof window !== 'undefined' && window.location.port === '3000'
    43→    const socketUrl = isDev
    44→      ? `${window.location.protocol}//${window.location.hostname}:8000`
    45→      : undefined  // undefined = same origin (through nginx)
    46→
    47→    const socket = io(socketUrl, {
    48→      path: '/socket.io/',
    49→      auth: { token: session.accessToken },
    50→      transports: ['polling', 'websocket'],
    51→      reconnection: true,
    52→      reconnectionAttempts: Infinity,
    53→      reconnectionDelay: 1000,
    54→      reconnectionDelayMax: 30000,
    55→    })
    56→
    57→    socketRef.current = socket
    58→
    59→    socket.on('connect', () => {
    60→      setIsConnected(true)
    61→    })
    62→
    63→    socket.on('disconnect', () => {
    64→      setIsConnected(false)
    65→    })
    66→
    67→    socket.on('connected', () => {
    68→      // Server confirmed JWT authentication
    69→    })
    70→
    71→    socket.on('incident_created', (data: WebSocketEvent) => {
    72→      invalidateIncidentQueries()
    73→      invalidateNotificationQueries()
    74→
    75→      const severityToast = data.severity === 'critical' ? 'error' : data.severity === 'warning' ? 'warning' : 'info'
    76→      toast.showToast(severityToast, `New incident: ${data.title}`, 6000)
    77→    })
    78→
    79→    socket.on('incident_resolved', (data: WebSocketEvent) => {
    80→      invalidateIncidentQueries()
    81→      invalidateNotificationQueries()
    82→
    83→      toast.success(`Resolved: ${data.title}`, 5000)
    84→    })
    85→
    86→    socket.on('incident_acknowledged', (data: WebSocketEvent) => {
    87→      invalidateIncidentQueries()
    88→      invalidateNotificationQueries()
    89→    })
    90→
    91→    socket.on('notification', () => {
    92→      invalidateNotificationQueries()
    93→    })
    94→
    95→    socket.on('connect_error', () => {
    96→      setIsConnected(false)
    97→    })
    98→
    99→    return () => {
   100→      socket.disconnect()
   101→      socketRef.current = null
   102→      setIsConnected(false)
   103→    }
   104→  }, [session?.accessToken, sessionStatus, invalidateIncidentQueries, invalidateNotificationQueries, toast])
   105→
   106→  return { isConnected }
   107→}
   108→

"""Real-time event service using Redis pub/sub.

This service bridges business logic events to connected WebSocket clients.
Events are published to a Redis channel and picked up by the Socket.IO
server, which forwards them to connected users.
"""

import json
import logging
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional

from app.core.config import settings

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

EVENTS_CHANNEL = "bit:events"


class RealtimeService:
    """Publishes events to Redis pub/sub for WebSocket distribution."""

    @staticmethod
    async def publish_event(
        event_type: str,
        data: Dict[str, Any],
        target_user_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
    ) -> None:
        """Publish an event to Redis for real-time distribution.

        Args:
            event_type: Event name (e.g. incident_created, notification)
            data: JSON-serializable payload
            target_user_ids: If None, broadcast to all; if list, only those users
        """
        try:
            import redis.asyncio as aioredis

            r = aioredis.from_url(settings.REDIS_URL)
            message = json.dumps({
                "event": event_type,
                "data": data,
                "target_user_ids": target_user_ids,
            })
            await r.publish(EVENTS_CHANNEL, message)
            await r.aclose()
            logger.debug(f"Published event '{event_type}' to Redis")
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Failed to publish event '{event_type}': {e}")

    @staticmethod
    def publish_event_sync(
        event_type: str,
        data: Dict[str, Any],
        target_user_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
    ) -> None:
        """Synchronous version for use in Celery workers.

        Args:
            event_type: Event name
            data: JSON-serializable payload
            target_user_ids: If None, broadcast to all; if list, only those users
        """
        try:
            import redis

            r = redis.from_url(settings.REDIS_URL)
            message = json.dumps({
                "event": event_type,
                "data": data,
                "target_user_ids": target_user_ids,
            })
            r.publish(EVENTS_CHANNEL, message)
            r.close()
            logger.debug(f"Published event '{event_type}' to Redis (sync)")
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Failed to publish event '{event_type}' (sync): {e}")

"""API endpoints for incident management.

View, acknowledge, and resolve incidents.
"""

import logging
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import Optional

from fastapi import APIRouter, Depends, HTTPException, Query, status
from sqlalchemy import and_, func, select
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from sqlalchemy.orm import selectinload

from app.api.deps import get_current_admin_user, get_current_superadmin_user, get_current_user, get_db
from app.core.audit import log_audit_event
from app.models.audit_log import AuditAction
from app.models.incident import Incident, IncidentStatus
from app.models.notification_rule import NotificationRule
from app.models.user import User
from app.services.alert_service import send_acknowledge_notification, send_resolution_notification
from app.schemas.incident import (
    BulkActionResult,
    BulkIncidentAction,
    IncidentAcknowledge,
    IncidentListResponse,
    IncidentResolve,
    IncidentResponse,
    IncidentSummary,
    IncidentUpdate,
)

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

router = APIRouter()


def _incident_to_response(incident: Incident) -> IncidentResponse:
    """Convert Incident model to response schema with relationships."""
    return IncidentResponse(
        id=incident.id,
        title=incident.title,
        description=incident.description,
        status=incident.status,
        severity=incident.severity,
        trigger_type=incident.trigger_type,
        resource_type=incident.resource_type,
        resource_id=incident.resource_id,
        details=incident.details,
        notification_rule_id=incident.notification_rule_id,
        notification_rule_name=incident.notification_rule.name if incident.notification_rule else None,
        started_at=incident.started_at,
        acknowledged_at=incident.acknowledged_at,
        acknowledged_by=incident.acknowledged_by,
        acknowledged_by_name=incident.acknowledger.full_name if incident.acknowledger else None,
        resolved_at=incident.resolved_at,
        resolved_by=incident.resolved_by,
        resolved_by_name=incident.resolver.full_name if incident.resolver else None,
        notes=incident.notes,
    )

from fastapi import Depends, HTTPException, status
from fastapi.security import HTTPBearer, HTTPAuthorizationCredentials
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession
from sqlalchemy import select

from app.core.database import get_db
from app.core.security import decode_token
from app.core.token_blacklist import is_token_blacklisted
from app.models.user import User, UserRole

security = HTTPBearer()


async def get_current_user(
    credentials: HTTPAuthorizationCredentials = Depends(security),
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
) -> User:
    """Get the current authenticated user.

    Requires full authentication including MFA verification (if enabled).
    """
    token = credentials.credentials
    payload = decode_token(token)

    if payload is None:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="Invalid or expired token",
        )

    # Check if token has been revoked (logout, password change, etc.)
    if await is_token_blacklisted(token):
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="Token has been revoked",
        )

    if payload.get("type") != "access":
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="Invalid token type",
        )

    # Check MFA verification status
    # Tokens with mfa_verified=False are partial tokens pending MFA
    if payload.get("mfa_verified") is False:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="MFA verification required",
            headers={"X-MFA-Required": "true"},
        )

    user_id = payload.get("sub")
    if user_id is None:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="Invalid token payload",
        )

    result = await db.execute(select(User).where(User.id == int(user_id)))
    user = result.scalar_one_or_none()

    if user is None:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="User not found",
        )

    if not user.is_active:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
            detail="User is inactive",
        )

    return user


async def get_current_user_mfa_pending(
    credentials: HTTPAuthorizationCredentials = Depends(security),
    db: AsyncSession = Depends(get_db),
) -> User:
    """Get current user from a partial token (MFA pending).

    Used for the MFA verification endpoint. Accepts tokens with mfa_verified=False.
    """
    token = credentials.credentials
    payload = decode_token(token)

    if payload is None:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="Invalid or expired token",
        )

    if payload.get("type") != "access":
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="Invalid token type",
        )

    user_id = payload.get("sub")
    if user_id is None:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="Invalid token payload",
        )

    result = await db.execute(select(User).where(User.id == int(user_id)))
    user = result.scalar_one_or_none()

    if user is None:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_401_UNAUTHORIZED,
            detail="User not found",
        )

    if not user.is_active:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
            detail="User is inactive",
        )

    return user


async def get_current_superadmin(
    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
) -> User:
    """Require the current user to be a superadmin."""
    if current_user.role != UserRole.SUPERADMIN:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
            detail="Superadmin access required",
        )
    return current_user


async def get_current_admin(
    current_user: User = Depends(get_current_user),
) -> User:
    """Require the current user to be an admin or superadmin."""
    if current_user.role not in [UserRole.SUPERADMIN, UserRole.ADMIN]:
        raise HTTPException(
            status_code=status.HTTP_403_FORBIDDEN,
            detail="Admin access required",
        )
    return current_user


# Alias for consistency
get_current_admin_user = get_current_admin
get_current_superadmin_user = get_current_superadmin

"""Pydantic schemas for security events."""
from pydantic import BaseModel
from datetime import datetime
from typing import Optional


class SecurityEventBase(BaseModel):

"""WAF log collector — queries CloudWatch Logs Insights for WAF BLOCK events.

Supports both CloudFront WAF (aws-waf-logs-concertacao) and
ALB WAF (aws-waf-logs-amazonia-alb) log groups.

CloudWatch Logs Insights is ASYNC: start_query + poll for completion.
Watermark cursor: queries only NEW events since last processed timestamp.
"""
import logging
import time
from datetime import datetime

from app.models.cloud_account import CloudAccount
from app.providers.aws.client import AWSClientFactory

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

CLOUDFRONT_LOG_GROUP = "aws-waf-logs-concertacao"
ALB_LOG_GROUP = "aws-waf-logs-amazonia-alb"
POLL_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 60

WAF_QUERY = """
fields @timestamp, action, terminatingRuleId,
       httpRequest.clientIp, httpRequest.uri, httpRequest.country,
       httpRequest.httpMethod,
       httpRequest.headers.0.value as user_agent
| filter action = 'BLOCK'
| sort @timestamp asc
| limit 10000
"""


class WafCollectorService:
    def __init__(self, cloud_account: CloudAccount):
        self.client_factory = AWSClientFactory(cloud_account)
        self.region = "sa-east-1"

    def query_cloudfront_waf(self, since_timestamp: datetime) -> list[dict]:
        return self._query_waf_logs(CLOUDFRONT_LOG_GROUP, since_timestamp)

    def query_alb_waf(self, since_timestamp: datetime) -> list[dict]:
        return self._query_waf_logs(ALB_LOG_GROUP, since_timestamp)

    def _query_waf_logs(self, log_group: str, since_timestamp: datetime) -> list[dict]:
        """Start a CloudWatch Logs Insights query and poll until complete."""
        logs_client = self.client_factory.get_client("logs", region=self.region)
        try:
            response = logs_client.start_query(
                logGroupName=log_group,
                startTime=int(since_timestamp.timestamp()),
                endTime=int(datetime.utcnow().timestamp()),
                queryString=WAF_QUERY,
            )
            query_id = response["queryId"]
            logger.info(f"Started CloudWatch query {query_id} on {log_group}")
            return self._poll_query(logs_client, query_id)
        except logs_client.exceptions.ResourceNotFoundException:
            logger.warning(f"Log group {log_group} not found — skipping")
            return []
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Failed to query {log_group}: {e}")
            return []

    def _poll_query(self, client, query_id: str) -> list[dict]:
        """Poll query until Complete, Failed, or timeout."""
        for _ in range(POLL_TIMEOUT_SECONDS):
            result = client.get_query_results(queryId=query_id)
            status = result["status"]
            if status == "Complete":
                logger.info(f"Query {query_id} complete: {len(result['results'])} rows")
                return result["results"]
            if status in ("Failed", "Cancelled"):
                logger.error(f"Query {query_id} ended with status: {status}")
                return []
            time.sleep(1)
        logger.warning(f"Query {query_id} timed out after {POLL_TIMEOUT_SECONDS}s")
        return []

"""Security service — aggregation queries for WAF attack data."""
import logging
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone
from typing import Optional

from sqlalchemy import func, select, text
from sqlalchemy.ext.asyncio import AsyncSession

from app.models.security_event import SecurityEvent
from app.schemas.security_event import AttackSummary, SecurityEventRead

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

_TOP_N = 20


class SecurityService:
    def __init__(self, db: AsyncSession):
        self.db = db

    async def get_attack_summary(self, hours: int = 24) -> AttackSummary:
        since = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(hours=hours)

        total = await self.db.scalar(
            select(func.count(SecurityEvent.id)).where(
                SecurityEvent.timestamp >= since
            )
        )

        top_rules = await self._top_n("rule_name", since)
        top_countries = await self._top_n("country_code", since)
        top_ips = await self._top_n("ip_address", since)
        top_asns = await self._top_n("ip_asn", since)
        sources = await self._top_n("source", since, limit=10)
        timeline = await self._timeline(since)

        return AttackSummary(
            period_hours=hours,
            total_blocks=total or 0,
            top_rules=top_rules,
            top_countries=top_countries,
            top_ips=top_ips,
            top_asns=top_asns,
            timeline=timeline,
            sources=sources,
        )

    async def _top_n(self, field: str, since: datetime, limit: int = _TOP_N) -> list[dict]:
        col = getattr(SecurityEvent, field)
        result = await self.db.execute(
            select(col, func.count(SecurityEvent.id).label("count"))
            .where(SecurityEvent.timestamp >= since, col.isnot(None))
            .group_by(col)
            .order_by(text("count DESC"))
            .limit(limit)
        )
        return [{"value": row[0], "count": row[1]} for row in result.all()]

    async def _timeline(self, since: datetime) -> list[dict]:
        """Try attack_timeline_1h continuous aggregate first, fallback to raw query."""
        try:
            result = await self.db.execute(
                text(
                    "SELECT hour, source, block_count "
                    "FROM attack_timeline_1h "
                    "WHERE hour >= :since "
                    "ORDER BY hour ASC"
                ),
                {"since": since},
            )
            rows = result.all()
            if rows:
                return [
                    {"hour": str(r.hour), "source": r.source, "block_count": r.block_count}
                    for r in rows
                ]
        except Exception:
            pass

        # Fallback: raw aggregation
        result = await self.db.execute(
            select(
                func.date_trunc("hour", SecurityEvent.timestamp).label("hour"),
                SecurityEvent.source,
                func.count(SecurityEvent.id).label("block_count"),
            )
            .where(SecurityEvent.timestamp >= since)
            .group_by(text("hour"), SecurityEvent.source)
            .order_by(text("hour ASC"))
        )
        return [
            {"hour": str(r.hour), "source": r.source, "block_count": r.block_count}
            for r in result.all()
        ]

    async def list_events(
        self,
        hours: int = 24,
        source: Optional[str] = None,
        rule: Optional[str] = None,
        page: int = 1,
        page_size: int = 100,
    ) -> dict:
        since = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(hours=hours)
        query = select(SecurityEvent).where(SecurityEvent.timestamp >= since)
        count_query = select(func.count(SecurityEvent.id)).where(
            SecurityEvent.timestamp >= since
        )

        if source:
            query = query.where(SecurityEvent.source == source)
            count_query = count_query.where(SecurityEvent.source == source)
        if rule:
            query = query.where(SecurityEvent.rule_name == rule)
            count_query = count_query.where(SecurityEvent.rule_name == rule)

        total = await self.db.scalar(count_query)
        offset = (page - 1) * page_size
        result = await self.db.execute(
            query.order_by(SecurityEvent.timestamp.desc())
            .offset(offset)
            .limit(page_size)
        )
        events = [
            SecurityEventRead.model_validate(e) for e in result.scalars().all()
        ]
        return {
            "items": [e.model_dump() for e in events],
            "total": total or 0,
            "page": page,
            "page_size": page_size,
        }

    async def get_top_attackers(self, hours: int = 24, limit: int = 20) -> list[dict]:
        since = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(hours=hours)
        result = await self.db.execute(
            select(
                SecurityEvent.ip_address,
                SecurityEvent.ip_org,
                SecurityEvent.ip_city,
                SecurityEvent.ip_asn,
                SecurityEvent.country_code,
                func.count(SecurityEvent.id).label("count"),
            )
            .where(
                SecurityEvent.timestamp >= since,
                SecurityEvent.ip_address.isnot(None),
            )
            .group_by(
                SecurityEvent.ip_address,
                SecurityEvent.ip_org,
                SecurityEvent.ip_city,
                SecurityEvent.ip_asn,
                SecurityEvent.country_code,
            )
            .order_by(text("count DESC"))
            .limit(limit)
        )
        return [
            {
                "ip": r.ip_address,
                "org": r.ip_org,
                "city": r.ip_city,
                "asn": r.ip_asn,
                "country": r.country_code,
                "count": r.count,
            }
            for r in result.all()
        ]

input.content:
"""Celery tasks for WAF log collection and attack spike detection.

collect_waf_events  — runs every 5 min, watermark cursor per source
detect_attack_spike — runs every 15 min, Z-score spike detection
"""
import logging
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone

from sqlalchemy import func, select
from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import insert as pg_insert

from app.core.sync_database import sync_db_session
from app.models.cloud_account import CloudAccount
from app.models.security_event import SecurityEvent
from app.services.ip_enrichment import IpEnrichmentService
from app.services.waf_collector import WafCollectorService
from workers.celery_app import celery_app

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def _get_cloud_account(db) -> CloudAccount:
    """Fetch the active AWS CloudAccount (first active one for now)."""
    from app.core.config import settings

    account_id = getattr(settings, "CONCERTACAO_AWS_ACCOUNT_ID", None)
    if account_id:
        account = db.execute(
            select(CloudAccount).where(CloudAccount.id == int(account_id))
        ).scalar_one_or_none()
        if account:
            return account

    # Fallback: first active AWS account
    from app.models.cloud_account import CloudProvider
    account = db.execute(
        select(CloudAccount).where(
            CloudAccount.provider == CloudProvider.AWS,
            CloudAccount.is_active == True,
        ).limit(1)
    ).scalar_one_or_none()

    if not account:
        raise RuntimeError("No active AWS CloudAccount found in database")
    return account


def _parse_events(raw_events: list, source: str) -> list[dict]:
    """Convert CloudWatch Logs Insights result rows to dicts for upsert.

    CloudWatch Insights format: [{'field': '@timestamp', 'value': '...'}, ...]
    """
    parsed = []
    for event in raw_events:
        fields = {f["field"]: f["value"] for f in event}
        ts_raw = fields.get("@timestamp", "")
        if not ts_raw:
            continue
        try:
            ts = datetime.fromisoformat(ts_raw.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
        except ValueError:
            continue
        parsed.append({
            "timestamp": ts,
            "source": source,
            "rule_name": fields.get("terminatingRuleId"),
            "action": fields.get("action", "BLOCK"),
            "ip_address": fields.get("httpRequest.clientIp"),
            "user_agent": fields.get("user_agent"),
            "uri": fields.get("httpRequest.uri"),
            "country_code": fields.get("httpRequest.country"),
            "http_method": fields.get("httpRequest.httpMethod"),
            "ip_org": None,
            "ip_city": None,
            "ip_asn": None,
        })
    return parsed


@celery_app.task(name="workers.tasks.waf_collector.collect_waf_events")
def collect_waf_events():
    """Collect WAF BLOCK events from CloudFront and ALB WAF logs.

    Uses watermark cursor per source (not a fixed 6h window) to avoid
    re-processing already-seen events. Upserts with on_conflict_do_nothing
    for safe idempotency.
    """
    with sync_db_session() as db:
        try:
            cloud_account = _get_cloud_account(db)
        except RuntimeError as e:
            logger.error(f"collect_waf_events: {e}")
            return

        collector = WafCollectorService(cloud_account)
        enricher = IpEnrichmentService()

        for source, fetch_fn in [
            ("waf_cloudfront", collector.query_cloudfront_waf),
            ("waf_alb", collector.query_alb_waf),
        ]:
            last_ts = db.execute(
                select(func.max(SecurityEvent.timestamp)).where(
                    SecurityEvent.source == source
                )
            ).scalar()

            since = last_ts if last_ts else (
                datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(hours=6)
            )

            logger.info(f"Fetching {source} events since {since.isoformat()}")
            raw = fetch_fn(since_timestamp=since)
            if not raw:
                logger.info(f"No new events for {source}")
                continue

            parsed = _parse_events(raw, source)
            if not parsed:
                continue

            # Enrich IPs
            unique_ips = list({e["ip_address"] for e in parsed if e.get("ip_address")})
            enrichments = enricher.enrich_batch(unique_ips)

            for event in parsed:
                ip = event.get("ip_address")
                if ip and ip in enrichments:
                    geo = enrichments[ip]
                    event["ip_org"] = geo.get("org")
                    event["ip_city"] = geo.get("city")
                    event["ip_asn"] = geo.get("as")
                    if not event.get("country_code"):
                        event["country_code"] = geo.get("countryCode")

            stmt = (
                pg_insert(SecurityEvent)
                .values(parsed)
                .on_conflict_do_nothing()
            )
            db.execute(stmt)
            logger.info(f"Upserted {len(parsed)} {source} events")

        db.commit()

        # Publish real-time event so frontend refreshes
        from app.services.realtime import RealtimeService
        RealtimeService.publish_event_sync(
            event_type="security_event",
            data={"timestamp": datetime.utcnow().isoformat()},
            target_user_ids=None,
        )


@celery_app.task(name="workers.tasks.waf_collector.detect_attack_spike")
def detect_attack_spike():
    """Detect sudden attack spikes: current 15-min window vs. 7-day baseline + 3σ.

    Fires a SECURITY_ATTACK alert via SyncAlertService when spike detected.
    """
    with sync_db_session() as db:
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        window_start = now - timedelta(minutes=15)
        baseline_start = now - timedelta(days=7)

        current_count = db.execute(
            select(func.count(SecurityEvent.id)).where(
                SecurityEvent.timestamp >= window_start
            )
        ).scalar() or 0

        # Baseline: count per 15-min bucket over last 7 days
        from sqlalchemy import text
        baseline_rows = db.execute(
            text(
                "SELECT count(*) as cnt FROM security_events "
                "WHERE timestamp BETWEEN :start AND :end "
                "GROUP BY floor(extract(epoch from timestamp) / 900)"
            ),
            {"start": baseline_start, "end": window_start},
        ).fetchall()

        if not baseline_rows:
            logger.info("detect_attack_spike: insufficient baseline data")
            return

        counts = [row[0] for row in baseline_rows]
        n = len(counts)
        mean = sum(counts) / n
        variance = sum((c - mean) ** 2 for c in counts) / n
        stddev = variance ** 0.5

        threshold = mean + 3 * stddev
        logger.info(
            f"Attack spike check: current={current_count}, "
            f"baseline mean={mean:.1f}, 3σ threshold={threshold:.1f}"
        )

        if current_count > threshold and current_count > 10:
            # Find top rule and IP in current window
            top_rule_row = db.execute(
                select(SecurityEvent.rule_name, func.count(SecurityEvent.id).label("cnt"))
                .where(SecurityEvent.timestamp >= window_start)
                .group_by(SecurityEvent.rule_name)
                .order_by(text("cnt DESC"))
                .limit(1)
            ).first()
            top_ip_row = db.execute(
                select(SecurityEvent.ip_address, func.count(SecurityEvent.id).label("cnt"))
                .where(SecurityEvent.timestamp >= window_start)
                .group_by(SecurityEvent.ip_address)
                .order_by(text("cnt DESC"))
                .limit(1)
            ).first()

            top_rule = top_rule_row[0] if top_rule_row else "unknown"
            top_ip = top_ip_row[0] if top_ip_row else "unknown"

            try:
                from app.models.notification_rule import Severity, TriggerType
                from app.services.alert_service_sync import SyncAlertService

                alert_service = SyncAlertService(db)
                alert_service.trigger_alert(
                    trigger_type=TriggerType.SECURITY_ATTACK,
                    severity=Severity.CRITICAL,
                    title=f"Attack Spike: {current_count} bloqueios em 15min",
                    message=(
                        f"Detectado pico de ataques: {current_count} bloqueios em 15 min "
                        f"(baseline: {mean:.0f} ± {stddev:.0f}). "
                        f"Regra principal: {top_rule}. IP principal: {top_ip}."
                    ),
                    resource_type="waf",
                    resource_id="concertacao",
                    details={
                        "current_count": current_count,
                        "baseline_mean": round(mean, 1),
                        "baseline_stddev": round(stddev, 1),
                        "threshold": round(threshold, 1),
                        "top_rule": top_rule,
                        "top_ip": top_ip,
                    },
                )
                logger.warning(

"""Celery tasks for WAF log collection and attack spike detection.

collect_waf_events  — runs every 5 min, watermark cursor per source
detect_attack_spike — runs every 15 min, Z-score spike detection
"""
import logging
from datetime import datetime, timedelta, timezone

from sqlalchemy import func, select
from sqlalchemy.dialects.postgresql import insert as pg_insert

from app.core.sync_database import sync_db_session
from app.models.cloud_account import CloudAccount
from app.models.security_event import SecurityEvent
from app.services.ip_enrichment import IpEnrichmentService
from app.services.waf_collector import WafCollectorService
from workers.celery_app import celery_app

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


def _get_cloud_account(db) -> CloudAccount:
    """Fetch the active AWS CloudAccount (first active one for now)."""
    from app.core.config import settings

    account_id = getattr(settings, "CONCERTACAO_AWS_ACCOUNT_ID", None)
    if account_id:
        account = db.execute(
            select(CloudAccount).where(CloudAccount.id == int(account_id))
        ).scalar_one_or_none()
        if account:
            return account

    # Fallback: first active AWS account
    from app.models.cloud_account import CloudProvider
    account = db.execute(
        select(CloudAccount).where(
            CloudAccount.provider == CloudProvider.AWS,
            CloudAccount.is_active == True,
        ).limit(1)
    ).scalar_one_or_none()

    if not account:
        raise RuntimeError("No active AWS CloudAccount found in database")
    return account


def _parse_events(raw_events: list, source: str) -> list[dict]:
    """Convert CloudWatch Logs Insights result rows to dicts for upsert.

    CloudWatch Insights format: [{'field': '@timestamp', 'value': '...'}, ...]
    """
    parsed = []
    for event in raw_events:
        fields = {f["field"]: f["value"] for f in event}
        ts_raw = fields.get("@timestamp", "")
        if not ts_raw:
            continue
        try:
            ts = datetime.fromisoformat(ts_raw.replace("Z", "+00:00"))
        except ValueError:
            continue
        parsed.append({
            "timestamp": ts,
            "source": source,
            "rule_name": fields.get("terminatingRuleId"),
            "action": fields.get("action", "BLOCK"),
            "ip_address": fields.get("httpRequest.clientIp"),
            "user_agent": fields.get("user_agent"),
            "uri": fields.get("httpRequest.uri"),
            "country_code": fields.get("httpRequest.country"),
            "http_method": fields.get("httpRequest.httpMethod"),
            "ip_org": None,
            "ip_city": None,
            "ip_asn": None,
        })
    return parsed


@celery_app.task(name="workers.tasks.waf_collector.collect_waf_events")
def collect_waf_events():
    """Collect WAF BLOCK events from CloudFront and ALB WAF logs.

    Uses watermark cursor per source (not a fixed 6h window) to avoid
    re-processing already-seen events. Upserts with on_conflict_do_nothing
    for safe idempotency.
    """
    with sync_db_session() as db:
        try:
            cloud_account = _get_cloud_account(db)
        except RuntimeError as e:
            logger.error(f"collect_waf_events: {e}")
            return

        collector = WafCollectorService(cloud_account)
        enricher = IpEnrichmentService()

        for source, fetch_fn in [
            ("waf_cloudfront", collector.query_cloudfront_waf),
            ("waf_alb", collector.query_alb_waf),
        ]:
            last_ts = db.execute(
                select(func.max(SecurityEvent.timestamp)).where(
                    SecurityEvent.source == source
                )
            ).scalar()

            since = last_ts if last_ts else (
                datetime.now(timezone.utc) - timedelta(hours=6)
            )

            logger.info(f"Fetching {source} events since {since.isoformat()}")
            raw = fetch_fn(since_timestamp=since)
            if not raw:
                logger.info(f"No new events for {source}")
                continue

            parsed = _parse_events(raw, source)
            if not parsed:
                continue

            # Enrich IPs
            unique_ips = list({e["ip_address"] for e in parsed if e.get("ip_address")})
            enrichments = enricher.enrich_batch(unique_ips)

            for event in parsed:
                ip = event.get("ip_address")
                if ip and ip in enrichments:
                    geo = enrichments[ip]
                    event["ip_org"] = geo.get("org")
                    event["ip_city"] = geo.get("city")
                    event["ip_asn"] = geo.get("as")
                    if not event.get("country_code"):
                        event["country_code"] = geo.get("countryCode")

            stmt = (
                pg_insert(SecurityEvent)
                .values(parsed)
                .on_conflict_do_nothing()
            )
            db.execute(stmt)
            logger.info(f"Upserted {len(parsed)} {source} events")

        db.commit()

        # Publish real-time event so frontend refreshes
        from app.services.realtime import RealtimeService
        RealtimeService.publish_event_sync(
            event_type="security_event",
            data={"timestamp": datetime.utcnow().isoformat()},
            target_user_ids=None,
        )


@celery_app.task(name="workers.tasks.waf_collector.detect_attack_spike")
def detect_attack_spike():
    """Detect sudden attack spikes: current 15-min window vs. 7-day baseline + 3σ.

    Fires a SECURITY_ATTACK alert via SyncAlertService when spike detected.
    """
    with sync_db_session() as db:
        now = datetime.now(timezone.utc)
        window_start = now - timedelta(minutes=15)
        baseline_start = now - timedelta(days=7)

        current_count = db.execute(
            select(func.count(SecurityEvent.id)).where(
                SecurityEvent.timestamp >= window_start
            )
        ).scalar() or 0

        # Baseline: count per 15-min bucket over last 7 days
        from sqlalchemy import text
        baseline_rows = db.execute(
            text(
                "SELECT count(*) as cnt FROM security_events "
                "WHERE timestamp BETWEEN :start AND :end "
                "GROUP BY floor(extract(epoch from timestamp) / 900)"
            ),
            {"start": baseline_start, "end": window_start},
        ).fetchall()

        if not baseline_rows:
            logger.info("detect_attack_spike: insufficient baseline data")
            return

        counts = [row[0] for row in baseline_rows]
        n = len(counts)
        mean = sum(counts) / n
        variance = sum((c - mean) ** 2 for c in counts) / n
        stddev = variance ** 0.5

        threshold = mean + 3 * stddev
        logger.info(
            f"Attack spike check: current={current_count}, "
            f"baseline mean={mean:.1f}, 3σ threshold={threshold:.1f}"
        )

        if current_count > threshold and current_count > 10:
            # Find top rule and IP in current window
            top_rule_row = db.execute(
                select(SecurityEvent.rule_name, func.count(SecurityEvent.id).label("cnt"))
                .where(SecurityEvent.timestamp >= window_start)
                .group_by(SecurityEvent.rule_name)
                .order_by(text("cnt DESC"))
                .limit(1)
            ).first()
            top_ip_row = db.execute(
                select(SecurityEvent.ip_address, func.count(SecurityEvent.id).label("cnt"))
                .where(SecurityEvent.timestamp >= window_start)
                .group_by(SecurityEvent.ip_address)
                .order_by(text("cnt DESC"))
                .limit(1)
            ).first()

            top_rule = top_rule_row[0] if top_rule_row else "unknown"
            top_ip = top_ip_row[0] if top_ip_row else "unknown"

            try:
                from app.models.notification_rule import Severity, TriggerType
                from app.services.alert_service_sync import SyncAlertService

                alert_service = SyncAlertService(db)
                alert_service.trigger_alert(
                    trigger_type=TriggerType.SECURITY_ATTACK,
                    severity=Severity.CRITICAL,
                    title=f"Attack Spike: {current_count} bloqueios em 15min",
                    message=(
                        f"Detectado pico de ataques: {current_count} bloqueios em 15 min "
                        f"(baseline: {mean:.0f} ± {stddev:.0f}). "
                        f"Regra principal: {top_rule}. IP principal: {top_ip}."
                    ),
                    resource_type="waf",
                    resource_id="concertacao",
                    details={
                        "current_count": current_count,
                        "baseline_mean": round(mean, 1),
                        "baseline_stddev": round(stddev, 1),
                        "threshold": round(threshold, 1),
                        "top_rule": top_rule,
                        "top_ip": top_ip,
                    },
                )
                logger.warning(
                    f"ATTACK SPIKE detected: {current_count} blocks in 15min "
                    f"(threshold {threshold:.0f})"
                )
            except Exception as e:
                logger.error(f"Failed to trigger attack spike alert: {e}")

"""Notification Rule model for alert triggers."""
import enum
from sqlalchemy import (
    Column,
    Integer,
    String,
    Boolean,
    Text,
    DateTime,
    ForeignKey,
    Enum,
    JSON,
)
from sqlalchemy.orm import relationship
from sqlalchemy.sql import func

from app.core.database import Base


class TriggerType(str, enum.Enum):
    """Types of events that can trigger notifications."""
    MONITOR_DOWN = "monitor_down"
    MONITOR_UP = "monitor_up"
    MONITOR_DEGRADED = "monitor_degraded"
    SSL_EXPIRING = "ssl_expiring"
    SSL_EXPIRED = "ssl_expired"
    RESOURCE_UNHEALTHY = "resource_unhealthy"
    RESOURCE_CREATED = "resource_created"
    RESOURCE_DELETED = "resource_deleted"
    DISCOVERY_COMPLETE = "discovery_complete"
    DISCOVERY_FAILED = "discovery_failed"
    BUDGET_THRESHOLD = "budget_threshold"
    BUDGET_EXCEEDED = "budget_exceeded"
    HOST_METRIC_THRESHOLD = "host_metric_threshold"
    CONTAINER_METRIC_THRESHOLD = "container_metric_threshold"
    CLOUDWATCH_METRIC_THRESHOLD = "cloudwatch_metric_threshold"


class Severity(str, enum.Enum):
    """Alert severity levels."""
    CRITICAL = "critical"
    WARNING = "warning"
    INFO = "info"


class NotificationRule(Base):
    """
    Notification rules define when and how to send alerts.

    Each rule specifies:
    - What event triggers the notification (trigger_type)
    - What severity levels to match (severity_filter)
    - Which channels to notify (channel_ids)
    - Optional conditions (e.g., specific monitors, resources)
    """
    __tablename__ = "notification_rules"

    id = Column(Integer, primary_key=True, index=True)
    name = Column(String(255), nullable=False)
    description = Column(Text, nullable=True)

    # Trigger configuration
    trigger_type = Column(Enum(TriggerType), nullable=False)
    severity_filter = Column(JSON, nullable=True)  # List of severities to match, null = all

    # Conditions (optional filters)
    conditions = Column(JSON, nullable=True)
    # Example conditions:
    # {
    #   "monitor_ids": [1, 2, 3],  # Only for specific monitors
    #   "resource_types": ["ec2", "rds"],  # Only for specific resource types
    #   "cloud_account_ids": [1],  # Only for specific accounts
    #   "ssl_days_threshold": 14,  # SSL expiring within X days
    # }

    # Channels to notify (list of channel IDs)
    channel_ids = Column(JSON, nullable=False)  # List of notification_channel IDs

    # Notification settings
    cooldown_minutes = Column(Integer, default=0)  # Min time between repeated alerts (0 = disabled, rely on threshold)
    include_details = Column(Boolean, default=True)  # Include full details in notification

    # Custom message templates (optional - uses defaults if not set)
    # Supports variables like {{monitor_name}}, {{status}}, {{url}}, {{error}}, etc.
    message_subject = Column(String(500), nullable=True)  # Email subject / notification title
    message_body = Column(Text, nullable=True)  # Full message body (supports HTML for email)

    # Status
    is_active = Column(Boolean, default=True, nullable=False, index=True)
    last_triggered = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), nullable=True)
    trigger_count = Column(Integer, default=0)

    # Timestamps
    created_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), server_default=func.now())
    updated_at = Column(DateTime(timezone=True), onupdate=func.now())

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"<NotificationRule(id={self.id}, name='{self.name}', trigger='{self.trigger_type}')>"
